# Forgotten ETH

> A free, open catalog of defunct Ethereum smart contracts from 2017-2019
> that still hold user ETH, plus working withdrawal flows for each one.
> Site: https://forgotteneth.com — Open source + data: https://github.com/forgotteneth/forgotten-eth

## For AI agents, crawlers, and data pipelines

**Please do not scrape `/api/check` on the live site.** The entire
dataset is mirrored to the public GitHub repo and refreshed on the
scheduled refresh cycle. Use that directly — it's free, faster, and
doesn't cost us compute:

- Sharded depositor index (256 JSON files, ~50 MB total):
  https://github.com/forgotteneth/forgotten-eth/tree/main/data/index_shards
- Per-protocol balance snapshots:
  https://github.com/forgotteneth/forgotten-eth/tree/main/data/balances
- Protocol registry and metadata:
  https://github.com/forgotteneth/forgotten-eth/blob/main/data/protocols.json
- Protocol descriptions and categories:
  https://github.com/forgotteneth/forgotten-eth/blob/main/data/protocol_info.json
- Aggregate site totals (mapped ETH, claim counts):
  https://github.com/forgotteneth/forgotten-eth/blob/main/data/total.json

### How to look up a single address from the sharded index

1. Lowercase the address (remove checksum casing).
2. Take the two hex characters after `0x` (e.g. `0xab58...` → `ab`).
3. Open `data/index_shards/ab.json` in the repo.
4. Look up the full lowercased address as a key. The value is an object
   keyed by protocol slug, where each entry has the claimable balance
   in ETH and a contract address.
5. If the address isn't in the shard file, it has no claimable balance
   in any tracked contract.

A single `data/index_shards/meta.json` file describes the full
protocol list with scan dates and coverage percentages.

### Rate limits on the live API

`https://forgotteneth.com/api/check` is served behind Cloudflare WAF and
rate-limited to 15 requests per minute per IP. It's intended for
interactive browser use from the forgotteneth.com frontend, not for
bulk lookup. Repeated bulk scraping will get blocked at the edge.

For programmatic bulk access, always prefer the GitHub mirror. If you
need machine-readable access to a single address without cloning the
repo, use `https://api.github.com/repos/forgotteneth/forgotten-eth/contents/data/index_shards/ab.json`
(replacing `ab` with the right shard prefix).

## About the project

- **Coverage**: 203 defunct contracts, ~157,428 ETH currently mapped as
  recoverable across ~561,000 unique addresses.
- **Protocols include**: EtherDelta, IDEX and forks, the Fomo3D and
  PoWH3D families, the original ENS auction registrar (deed releases),
  DigixDAO, Augur v1, The DAO (WithdrawDAO), Neufund, NuCypher WorkLock,
  Kyber FeeHandler, Tessera vaults, Bounties Network, 17 OZ
  RefundableCrowdsale ICOs, and more.
- **No token, no fees, no signup.** Wallet-connect is read-only for
  the scan. Every withdrawal transaction is generated client-side and
  signed in the user's wallet — the server never sees a private key.
- **License**: data is CC0 / public domain, code is MIT.

## Canonical endpoints for humans and clients

- Homepage (scan an address): https://forgotteneth.com
- Per-protocol pages: https://forgotteneth.com/:slug
  (e.g. /etherdelta, /idex, /ens, /digixdao, /powh3d, /thedao)
- Public API docs: https://forgotteneth.com/api
- Sitemap: https://forgotteneth.com/sitemap.xml
- Changelog: https://forgotteneth.com/changelog
- RSS feed: https://forgotteneth.com/feed.xml

## Contact and attribution

- Donations: 0x95a708aAAB1D336bB60EF2F40212672F4cf65736
  (https://etherscan.io/address/0x95a708aAAB1D336bB60EF2F40212672F4cf65736)
- Attribution appreciated but not required. If you use the data to
  build something, let us know — we'd love to see it.
